text_original: Not to value and employ men of superior ability is the way to keep the people from rivalry among themselves;
text_normalized: Not to value and employ men of superior ability is the way to keep the people from rivalry among themselves;

text_original: not to prize articles which are difficult to procure is the way to keep them from becoming thieves;
text_normalized: not to prize articles which are difficult to procure is the way to keep them from becoming thieves;

text_original: not to show them what is likely to excite their desires is the way to keep their minds from disorder.
text_normalized: not to show them what is likely to excite their desires is the way to keep their minds from disorder.

text_original: Therefore the sage, in the exercise of his government, empties their minds, fills their bellies, weakens their wills, and strengthens their bones.
text_normalized: Therefore the sage, in the exercise of his government, empties their minds, fills their bellies, weakens their wills, and strengthens their bones.

text_original: He constantly (tries to) keep them without knowledge and without desire, and where there are those who have knowledge, to keep them from presuming to act (on it).
text_normalized: He constantly (tries to) keep them without knowledge and without desire, and where there are those who have knowledge, to keep them from presuming to act (on it).

text_original: When there is this abstinence from action, good order is universal.
text_normalized: When there is this abstinence from action, good order is universal.
